reviews.review_title: I assume a Buddhist would enjoy this.
reviews.review_description: It has statues and stuff to read and the incense was freshly lit.
Hi, we are the developers of this app. If you have any advice or suggestions for this game, you can post here. We will reply you as soon as possible.

So I like it, as a western Buddhist I’ve found it hard to find outlets for my spirituality. There is a book I’m assuming with mantras from the dhammapada in it. You’re sitting in front of a statue of Buddha and you can listen to monks doing mantras to meditate to. I enjoyed it, would like to see a couple more places maybe even from other temples, also English dhammapada translations would be amazing. Thank you for this, keep up the good work!
This game is a scam where everything you do suck your credit card dry for REAL.

developer_response.body: This is a special software for learning Buddha,not a game, this version we didn't do process well, after causing players to trigger the payment, the new version has been modified, thank you very much for your feedback!

reviews.review_description: I didn’t understand this app at all. I expected it to be a place to learn about Buddhism and practice meditation and prayer but I felt so out of place. There was no beginner aspect to this at all. Not a fan.

developer_response.body: This is a special software for learning Buddha,not a game, this version we didn't do process well, after causing players to trigger the payment, the new version has been modified, thank you very much for your feedback!

reviews.review_title: Waste of Time for English Speakers
reviews.review_description: As someone interested in Buddhism and wanting to practice and learn more, I found this app next to useless. There is a book to read and learn from but the lessons are in Chinese or Japanese or some other Asian language I can't read. Everything else is in English - POINTLESS. My other complaint is earning merit. You can offer fruit once a day for 50 points per day. When you accumulate 200 (read 4 days) you earn the privilege of BUYING an amulet. Another six days devotion earns you an opportunity to buy a view of the Pavilion. Visiting the Temple once a day builds a devotion but then earning the privilege to buy really cheapened the experience. The graphics are beautiful but the experience was a real disappointment.
